pattern: Sorting by start makes overlaps adjacent, so a single sweep can fold each interval into the previous one whenever they touch or cross.
hints: Two intervals can only merge if one begins before the other ends - and that relationship is easiest to see once they are in start order.
After sorting, compare each interval with the last one you committed to the output.
Sort by start. Keep a running interval; if the next start is within the running end, stretch the running end to the larger of the two ends, otherwise commit the running interval and start a new one.

edge_inputs.description: touching at a shared endpoint still merges
input not given in sorted start order
degenerate points and a spanning interval

stress.description: many random intervals forcing a real sort and merge pass
stress.note: overlaps occur frequently so merging actually happens
